_doc: User labels for the 113-row triage GREEN relabel CSV. Only cases that changed label are listed under 'changes'; the other 93 stayed GREEN and are tracked in 'agreements' for audit completeness.

changes.notes: PSA 6.5 in 62yo + enlarged prostate + hematuria (RBC 20-22 in urine). Even with normal DRE and reassuring free/total PSA ratio, PSA above age-cutoff plus persistent microhematuria requires urology referral within weeks for cystoscopy/repeat PSA.

Chest pressure radiating to jaw + dyspnea + tachycardia. Patient self-attributes to panic and cites recent normal MRI, but symptom pattern alone is the textbook cardiac-mimicking presentation. Labeling this GREEN trains the model to dismiss cardiac-pattern symptoms based on patient self-attribution.
Post-fall ankle pain x30 days now spreading proximally to knee. Spreading pain after trauma warrants imaging — YELLOW for ortho.

Elderly fall with head impact requiring staples; CT shows hypodensities + lacunar infarcts + volume loss. Ongoing post-trauma neuro monitoring + small-vessel disease findings warrant YELLOW for neuro follow-up, not GREEN home monitoring.

Right hand numbness x30 days, sleep-disrupting, painful, attributed to arthritis but other joints unaffected. Asymmetric persistent numbness needs nerve conduction / neuro evaluation — not GREEN with wrist brace.

12yo dog bite, mid-rabies-PEP series. Incomplete rabies PEP is an urgent compliance question — YELLOW for immediate vaccinator confirmation. Bite was from puppy not yet rabies-vaccinated at time of bite.
Pediatric ear-piercing bleeding recurring on multiple earring changes with infection signs. Recurrent bleeding + infection signs warrants YELLOW for in-person assessment.

Periorbital trauma with significant swelling, patient explicitly asking 'should I go to ER.' Even with intact vision and no LOC, periorbital fractures need imaging within 24-48h to rule out muscle entrapment.

Recurrent chest discomfort x365 days in 34yo despite 3 normal TMTs and Echos. Likely musculoskeletal but a YEAR of recurrent chest discomfort warrants at minimum YELLOW for one more structured workup.

Post wisdom-tooth extraction at day 4 with new fever, body aches, headache, fatigue. Patient explicitly asks about sepsis/endocarditis. Systemic symptoms 4 days post-procedure warrant YELLOW for medical (not just dental) evaluation.

14yo new-onset syncope with transient post-ictal arm immobility. Even if vasovagal, first syncope with focal neuro deficit warrants YELLOW for neuro/cardiology workup. The transient inability to move arms is the red flag the first doctor downplayed.

Diastolic 51 (with systolic 145) in patient on antihypertensives, asking whether to take usual dose tonight. Wide pulse pressure + low diastolic on meds is a real medication-management question. YELLOW for same-day GP/cardiology call.

Broken jaw screws + completely loosened bridge with exposed gums, prophylactic clindamycin prescribed. Patient choosing NOT to take antibiotics out of Stevens-Johnson fear and lives alone. Hardware failure + decision to refuse prophylaxis warrants YELLOW for in-person review.
Daily lightheadedness x3-6 months in adult, persistent, distressing. Chronic dizziness needs cardiac/neuro/ENT workup — YELLOW for outpatient evaluation.
New-onset palpitations with felt ectopy in 30yo, persistent x weeks, attributed to stress. New palpitations require ECG/Holter — YELLOW. Stress attribution does not rule out arrhythmia.
Pediatric fever 102-103°F x2 days. Pediatric high-fever needs YELLOW for in-person assessment, especially in regions with malaria/dengue risk.

Post-stroke patient with new behavioral changes + medication-induced excessive sleepiness. Behavioral change post-stroke can signal recurrent ischemia or mood disorder; oversedation in a stroke patient is its own risk.
AST + ALT + bilirubin all elevated x4 years (1460 days) with mild fatty liver. Persistent multi-LFT abnormality with bilirubin involvement warrants YELLOW for hepatology workup.

Post-hospital pneumonia patient now with worsening back pain + chest tightness. Recently hospitalized for pneumonia + new chest tightness is the exact pattern that should rule out PE / recurrent pneumonia / empyema — not GREEN with continued NSAIDs alone.

Recurrent angioedema-pattern lip + tongue swelling x90 days, food-triggered, no diagnosis from allergist or dentist. Recurrent partial lip/tongue swelling without identified trigger raises hereditary angioedema (HAE) or ACE-inhibitor-induced angioedema differential — risk of progression to airway involvement is what makes this YELLOW.
